revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわでのほききみのいらつめ 生年不詳 - [[622年]]）は[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]...'
revision.text:
'''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわでのほききみのいらつめ 生年不詳 - [[622年]]）は[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大郎（女）<ref>[[聖徳太子伝暦]]</ref>、高橋妃などとも呼ばれる。
== 出自 == 6世紀から7世紀に[[伴造]]として活躍した豪族[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref>[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]</ref>。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる<ref>平氏伝雑勘文所引上宮記</ref>。
== 経歴 == [[598年]]（[[推古天皇]]6年）聖徳太子の妃となる<ref>聖徳太子伝暦</ref>。四男四女を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった<ref>上宮聖徳法王帝説</ref>。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる<ref>聖徳太子伝暦</ref>。622年（推古天皇30年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子[[薨去]]の前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[磯長]]（しなが）墓に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020
== 出典 == <references />
{{Japanese-history-stub|かしわてのほききみのいらつめ}}
[[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|かしわてのほききみのいらつめ]] [[Category:622年没|かしわてのほききみのいらつめ]]

'''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわでのほききみのいらつめ 生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大郎（女）<ref>[[聖徳太子伝暦]]</ref>、高橋妃などとも呼ばれる。
== 出自 == 6世紀から7世紀に[[伴造]]として活躍した豪族[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref>[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]</ref>。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる<ref>平氏伝雑勘文所引上宮記</ref>。
== 経歴 == [[598年]]（推古天皇6年）聖徳太子の妃となる<ref>聖徳太子伝暦</ref>。四男四女を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった<ref>上宮聖徳法王帝説</ref>。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる<ref>聖徳太子伝暦</ref>。622年（推古天皇30年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子[[薨去]]の前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[磯長]]（しなが）墓に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020

revision.comment: 出典1件追加のついでに『』を付ける
revision.text:
'''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわでのほききみのいらつめ　生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大娘（女）『[[聖徳太子伝暦]]』、高橋妃『[[上宮太子拾遺記]]』所引『[[四天王寺障子伝]]』などとも書かれる。 == 出自 == 6世紀から7世紀に[[伴造]]として活躍した豪族[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[来目皇子|久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる『[[上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文]]』所引『[[上宮記]]』。 == 経歴 == 推古天皇6年（[[598年]]）聖徳太子の妃となる『聖徳太子伝暦』。四男四女『平氏伝雑勘文』所引『上宮記』は三男四女に作るを生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった『上宮聖徳法王帝説』。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる『聖徳太子伝暦』。推古天皇30年（622年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子が亡くなる前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[磯長陵]]（しながりょう）に合葬された[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘。 == 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020 == 出典 ==
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしわてのほききみのいらつめ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:622年没]]

'''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわでのほききみのいらつめ 生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大娘（女）<ref name="伝暦">『[[聖徳太子伝暦]]』</ref>、高橋妃<ref>『[[上宮太子拾遺記]]』所引『[[四天王寺障子伝]]』</ref>などとも書かれる。
== 出自 == [[6世紀]]から[[7世紀]]に[[伴造]]として活躍した[[豪族]]・[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref name="帝説">『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』</ref>。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[来目皇子|久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる<ref>『[[上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文]]』所引『[[上宮記]]』</ref>。
== 経歴 == 推古天皇6年（[[598年]]）聖徳太子の妃となる<ref name="伝暦"/>。四男四女<ref>『平氏伝雑勘文』所引『上宮記』は三男四女に作る</ref>を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった<ref name="帝説"/>。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる<ref name="伝暦"/>。推古天皇30年（622年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子が亡くなる前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[叡福寺|磯長陵]]（しながりょう）に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020

'''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわでのほききみのいらつめ 生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大娘（女）<ref name="伝暦">『[[聖徳太子伝暦]]』</ref>、高橋妃<ref>『[[上宮太子拾遺記]]』所引『[[四天王寺障子伝]]』</ref>などとも書かれる。
== 出自 == [[6世紀]]から[[7世紀]]に[[伴造]]として活躍した[[豪族]]・[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref name="帝説">『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』</ref>。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[来目皇子|久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる<ref>『[[上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文]]』所引『[[上宮記]]』</ref>。
== 経歴 == 推古天皇6年（[[598年]]）聖徳太子の妃となる<ref name="伝暦"/>。四男四女<ref>『平氏伝雑勘文』所引『上宮記』は三男四女に作る</ref>を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった<ref name="帝説"/>。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる<ref name="伝暦"/>。推古天皇30年（622年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子が亡くなる前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[叡福寺|磯長陵]]（しながりょう）に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020

{{参照方法|date=2021年3月}} '''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわで の ほききみのいらつめ、? - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大娘（女）<ref name="伝暦">『[[聖徳太子伝暦]]』</ref>・高橋妃<ref>『[[上宮太子拾遺記]]』所引『[[四天王寺障子伝]]』</ref>などとも書かれる。
== 出自 == [[6世紀]]から[[7世紀]]に[[伴造]]として活躍した[[豪族]]・[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref name="帝説">『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』</ref>。妹は聖徳太子の弟・[[来目皇子]]の夫人・膳比里古郎女<ref>『[[上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文]]』所引『[[上宮記]]』</ref>。
== 経歴 == [[推古天皇]]6年（[[598年]]）聖徳太子の妃になる<ref name="伝暦"/>。四男四女<ref>『平氏伝雑勘文』所引『上宮記』は三男四女に作る</ref>を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄・[[山背大兄王]]の妃になる<ref name="帝説"/>。聖徳太子は愛する'''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''に「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝わる<ref name="伝暦"/>。推古天皇30年（622年）聖徳太子と共に病となり陰暦2月21日に死去。翌2月22日に太子も死去する。聖徳太子の墓所である[[叡福寺|磯長陵]]（しながりょう）に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{参照方法|date=2021年3月}} '''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわで の ほききみのいらつめ、? - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の女性。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大娘（女）<ref name="伝暦">『[[聖徳太子伝暦]]』</ref>、高橋妃<ref>『[[上宮太子拾遺記]]』所引『[[四天王寺障子伝]]』</ref>などとも書かれる。
== 出自 == [[6世紀]]から[[7世紀]]に[[伴造]]として活躍した[[豪族]]・[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref name="帝説">『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』</ref>。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[来目皇子|久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる<ref>『[[上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文]]』所引『[[上宮記]]』</ref>。
== 経歴 == [[推古天皇]]6年（[[598年]]）聖徳太子の妃となる<ref name="伝暦"/>。四男四女<ref>『平氏伝雑勘文』所引『上宮記』は三男四女に作る</ref>を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった<ref name="帝説"/>。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる<ref name="伝暦"/>。推古天皇30年（622年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子が亡くなる前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[叡福寺|磯長陵]]（しながりょう）に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
{{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:6th-century Japanese female royalty]] [[Category:7th-century Japanese female royalty]] [[Category:Women of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Consorts of Japanese royalty]] [[Category:Prince Shōtoku|+Hokikimi]] [[Category:Kashiwade clan|Hokikimi no Iratsume]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]]

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
{{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.

She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. == References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (April 7, 622)) was a [[woman]] of the [[Asuka period]] and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|ja|上宮聖徳法王帝説|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|ja|山背大兄王|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (current-day {{ill|Eifukuji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女 up to rev 98176587
revision.text:
{{参照方法|date=2021年3月}} '''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわで の ほききみのいらつめ、? - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[女性]]。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大娘（女）<ref name="伝暦">『[[聖徳太子伝暦]]』</ref>、高橋妃<ref>『[[上宮太子拾遺記]]』所引『[[四天王寺障子伝]]』</ref>などとも書かれる。
== 出自 == [[6世紀]]から[[7世紀]]に[[伴造]]として活躍した[[豪族]]・[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref name="帝説">『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』</ref>。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[来目皇子|久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる<ref>『[[上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文]]』所引『[[上宮記]]』</ref>。
== 経歴 == [[推古天皇]]6年（[[598年]]）聖徳太子の妃となる<ref name="伝暦"/>。四男四女<ref>『平氏伝雑勘文』所引『上宮記』は三男四女に作る</ref>を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった<ref name="帝説"/>。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる<ref name="伝暦"/>。推古天皇30年（622年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子が亡くなる前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[叡福寺|磯長陵]]（しながりょう）に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{参照方法|date=2021年3月}} '''膳部菩岐々美郎女'''（かしわで の ほききみのいらつめ、? - [[推古天皇]]30年[[2月21日 (旧暦)|2月21日]]（[[622年]][[4月7日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[女性]]。[[聖徳太子]]の[[妃]]。膳大娘（女）<ref name="伝暦">『[[聖徳太子伝暦]]』</ref>、高橋妃<ref>『[[上宮太子拾遺記]]』所引『[[四天王寺障子伝]]』</ref>などとも書かれる。
== 出自 == [[6世紀]]から[[7世紀]]に[[伴造]]として活躍した[[豪族]]・[[膳氏]]の出身で、[[膳傾子|膳臣傾子]]（加多夫子とも、かしわでのおみかたぶこ）の娘<ref name="帝説">『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』</ref>。妹に聖徳太子の弟[[来目皇子|久米王]]の夫人となった膳比里古郎女がいる<ref>『[[上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文]]』所引『[[上宮記]]』</ref>。
== 経歴 == [[推古天皇]]6年（[[598年]]）聖徳太子の妃となる<ref name="伝暦"/>。四男四女<ref>『平氏伝雑勘文』所引『上宮記』は三男四女に作る</ref>を生んだが、そのうちの[[舂米女王]]（つきしねのひめみこ）は異母兄である[[山背大兄王]]の妃となった<ref name="帝説"/>。聖徳太子には信頼され、太子は「死後は共に埋葬されよう」と言ったと伝えられる<ref name="伝暦"/>。推古天皇30年（622年）聖徳太子と共に病となり、太子が亡くなる前日（旧暦2月21日）に没した。聖徳太子の墓所である[[叡福寺|磯長陵]]（しながりょう）に合葬された<ref>[[法隆寺]][[金堂]][[釈迦三尊像]][[光背]]銘</ref>。
== 参考文献 == * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』吉川弘文館、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * 竹内理三他編『日本古代人名辭典』第二巻、吉川弘文館、1959年、ISBN 4642020020
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0622|4|7}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Wakoku|qid=Q10888424}}|spouse={{ill|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972}}|children={{ill|Princess Tsukishine|qid=Q88919919}}; {{ill|Princess Umayako|qid=Q11667952}}|father={{ill|Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|qid=Q57887936}}|family={{ill|Kashiwade clan|qid=Q55529898}}|resting_place={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qid=Q48743902}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (April 7, 622)) was a [[:en:woman|woman]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and a consort of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|qq=|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|qq=|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|qq=|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|qq=|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|qq=|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|qq=|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|qq=|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|qq=|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.

She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|qq=|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.

She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|qq=|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|qq=|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|qq=|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|qq=|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|qq=|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0622|4|7}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Wakoku|WD=Q10888424}}|spouse={{ill|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972}}|children={{ill|Princess Tsukishine|WD=Q88919919}}; {{ill|Princess Umayako|WD=Q11667952}}|father={{ill|Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|WD=Q57887936}}|family={{ill|Kashiwade clan|WD=Q55529898}}|resting_place={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|WD=Q48743902}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (April 7, 622)) was a [[:en:woman|woman]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and a consort of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0622|4|7}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Wakoku|WD=Q10888424}}|spouse={{ill|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972}}|children={{ill|Princess Tsukishine|WD=Q88919919}}; {{ill|Princess Umayako|WD=Q11667952}}|father={{ill|Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|WD=Q57887936}}|family={{ill|Kashiwade clan|WD=Q55529898}}|resting_place={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|WD=Q48743902}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (April 7, 622)) was a [[:en:woman|woman]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and a consort of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0622|4|7}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Wakoku|WD=Q10888424}}|spouse={{ill|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972}}|children={{ill|Princess Tsukishine|WD=Q88919919}}; {{ill|Princess Umayako|WD=Q11667952}}|father={{ill|Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|WD=Q57887936}}|family={{ill|Kashiwade clan|WD=Q55529898}}|resting_place={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|WD=Q48743902}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (April 7, 622)) was a [[:en:woman|woman]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and a consort of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.
