She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''[[:en:Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu]]''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, [[:en:Prince Yamashiro|Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō]]<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifukuji]])<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:膳部菩岐々美郎女]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0622|4|7}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Wakoku|WD=Q10888424}}|spouse={{ill|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972}}|children={{ill|Princess Tsukishine|WD=Q88919919}}; {{ill|Princess Umayako|WD=Q11667952}}|father={{ill|Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|WD=Q57887936}}|family={{ill|Kashiwade clan|WD=Q55529898}}|resting_place={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|WD=Q48743902}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612414]] Wikidata name: Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (April 7, 622)) was a {{ill|woman|en|woman|WD=Q467|lt=woman}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|en|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|WD=Q3061873|lt=Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifukuji}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (April 7, 622)) was a {{ill|woman|en|woman|WD=Q467|lt=woman}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|en|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|WD=Q3061873|lt=Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu}}''</ref>. == Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifukuji}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (April 7, 622)) was a {{ill|woman|en|woman|WD=Q467|lt=woman}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Hokikimo no Iratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|en|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|WD=Q3061873|lt=Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=jouguuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifukuji}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} (April 7, 622)) was a {{ill|woman|en|woman|WD=Q467|lt=woman|1=Woman}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Oiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomonomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|en|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|WD=Q3061873|lt=Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|1=Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Prince Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|1=Prince Yamashiro}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48743902}} (current-day {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifukuji|1=Eifuku-ji}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2021}} '''Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume''' (膳部菩岐々美郎女, ? - {{ill|February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Lunisolar)|lt=February 21 (Japanese calendar)|ja|2月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 21 (Japanese calendar)|qq=}} in the 30th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} (April 7, 622)) was a {{ill|woman|en|woman|qid=Q467|lt=woman|1=Woman}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} and a consort of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. She is also referred to as {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja|膳大娘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōomi (female)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 膳部菩岐々美郎女|1=Kashiwade no Oiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612414}}<ref name="denryaku">''{{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|ja|聖徳太子伝暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|1=Shōtoku Taishi Denryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109359201}}''</ref> and {{ill|Takahashi Hi|ja|高橋妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja|四天王寺障子伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennōji Shōji Den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja|上宮太子拾遺記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Shūiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Ancestry == She was a member of the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55529898}}, a [[powerful clan]] active as {{ill|Tomobe|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomonomiyatsuko|qid=Q11381545}} in the 6th and 7th centuries. She was the daughter of {{ill|Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|ja|膳傾子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Omi Katabuko|1=Kashiwade-no-Katabuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q57887936}} (also known as Katabuko)<ref name="teisetu">''{{ill|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|en|Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|qid=Q3061873|lt=Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu|1=Jōgū Shōtoku Hōō Teisetsu}}''</ref>. Her younger sister, Kashiwade no Hiriko no Iratsume, became the consort of Prince {{ill|Kume no Ōji|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume no Ōji|1=Prince Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523737}}, a younger brother of Prince Shōtoku<ref>Quoted from ''{{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867356}}'' in ''{{ill|Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja|上宮太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgū Taishi Heishi Den Zakkanmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''</ref>.
== Biography == In the 6th year of Empress Suiko's reign (598), she became a consort of Prince Shōtoku<ref name="denryaku"/>. She bore four sons and four daughters<ref>The ''Heishi Den Zakkanmon'' quoting the ''Jōgūki'' mentions three sons and four daughters.</ref>. One of her daughters, {{ill|Tsukishine no Himemiko|ja|舂米女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukishine no Himemiko|1=Princess Tsukishine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q88919919}}, became a consort of her half-brother, {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|qid=Q3137695|lt=Yamashiro no Ōe no Ō|1=Prince Yamashiro}}<ref name="teisetu"/>. She was trusted by Prince Shōtoku, who reportedly said, "We shall be buried together after death."<ref name="denryaku"/> In the 30th year of Empress Suiko's reign (622), she fell ill alongside Prince Shōtoku and passed away on the day before his death (February 21, old calendar). She was interred with him in the {{ill|Shinaga Ryo|ja|磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Ryo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48743902}} (current-day {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|qid=Q11410732|lt=Eifukuji|1=Eifuku-ji}})<ref>Inscription on the halo of the {{ill|Shaka Triad|ja|釈迦三尊像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Triad|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦三尊|1=Shakyamuni Triad|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11071530}} in the {{ill|Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja|法隆寺金堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō (Hōryū-ji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>.
== References == * Sakamoto, Tarō & Hirano, Kunio (Eds.). ''Japanese Ancient Clan and Personal Name Dictionary.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1990. ISBN 4642022430. * Takeuchi, Risan (Ed.). ''Japanese Ancient Personal Name Dictionary, Vol. 2.'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1959. ISBN 4642020020.
